_about: Items from the Eanes History Center / Westbank Community Library District collection. Westlake History is the official site for this collection, with explicit permission from the Westbank Community Library District. Originals are held by Westbank CLD; high-resolution scans were produced by The Portal to Texas History (UNT Libraries) and are mirrored here under that permission.
_attribution: Eanes History Center, Westbank Community Library District. Digital reproductions originally produced by The Portal to Texas History, UNT Libraries.
_permission: Full reproduction and redistribution permission granted to Westlake History by the Westbank Community Library District as the official archive home for the EHC project.

items.title: Letter from Mary Mowinckle Johnson to Camila, February 13, 1975
items.summary: Photocopy of a letter from Mary Mowinckle Johnson to a woman named Camila. The letter goes into details about Johnson's time as a teacher at the Eanes Rock Schoolhouse and she also inquires about the Marshall family.

items.title: Notes from an Interview with Mike Molberg
items.summary: Typewritten notes from an interview with Mike Molberg about his time at Eanes School and a list of women who taught there.

items.title: Barton Family Tree and Bob Barton Obituary
items.summary: Photocopy of a family-tree chart for the Barton family with husband, wife, and children; also an obituary from the San Marcos Daily Record for Bob Barton Sr.

items.title: Photograph of Students
items.summary: Group of students standing outside leaning on a wire fence. Behind them: bleachers and a tree. Stamped 'APR 1940' on reverse.

items.title: Children in a Wagon
items.summary: Photograph of children in a wooden wagon; the child in the centre is holding a long pole and wearing a hat.

items.title: Eanes Rock Schoolhouse Class
items.summary: Eanes Rock Schoolhouse students seated at desks with the teacher at the back. Reverse: 'Kay Ort Orell (student). Old Rock school house, her 1956 class.'

items.title: Essay about Jackson Cemetery
items.summary: 'Jackson Cemetery, Travis County, Texas — Its Value as an Historical Landmark.' Covers creation of the cemetery, lot purchase, and interview material gathered by Melissa W.

items.title: Students in a Car Trunk
items.summary: Three Eanes Rock Schoolhouse students in the trunk of a car. Reverse marked 'R70'.

items.title: Eanes Rock Schoolhouse Students Outside
items.summary: Eanes Rock Schoolhouse students having lunch outside, sitting in front of a pile of rocks with trees beyond. '1930s Students having lunch in the Eanes Cafeteria' written on reverse in blue pen.

items.title: Students in a Wagon (Alamo Field Trip)
items.summary: Eanes Rock School House students on a field trip to San Antonio to see the Alamo, piled into a wagon stopped at a gas station. 'Eanes History Center' handwritten on reverse, 'R70' printed.

items.title: Photograph of Students Having Lunch
items.summary: Eanes Rock School House students about to eat lunch outside, lined up and looking towards the camera with trees in the background.

items.title: Four Eanes Rock Schoolhouse Students
items.summary: Four Eanes Rock Schoolhouse students standing in front of the Eanes Rock Schoolhouse building. Two girls in dresses, two boys in shirts and pants; a dog and a man bending down in the background.

items.title: Students at the Alamo
items.summary: Eanes Rock Schoolhouse students on a field trip to San Antonio, standing in front of the Alamo. Reverse marked 'R70'.

items.title: Handwritten Account of the Boat Parade
items.summary: Handwritten account by Mary Mowinckle of the July 4, 1981 Boat Parade on Lake Austin, written for the Neighborhood Section of the Austin American-Statesman.

items.title: Essay by Laura Castro (Dellana Family)
items.summary: Essay on how the Dellana family came to Rollingwood. Castro covers Condado Dellana's emigration from Italy to Texas, his work building the capitol building, and the cave on his Rollingwood property whose bat-guano fertilizer he sold to nurseries across the state.

items.title: Illustration of Lost Creek Log Cabin
items.summary: Art print of the Lost Creek Log Cabin in disrepair: stone chimney falling apart, wooden structure failing. Captioned 'Lost Creek Log Cabin circa 1872'. Numbered 69/100; signed Curtis.

items.title: Students in a Wagon
items.summary: Eanes Rock Schoolhouse students on a wooden wagon; one student climbing in, others looking off frame. Stone building and trees in the background. Reverse marked 'R70'.

items.title: Blue Book Essay about the Beard Family
items.summary: Essay about the Beard family contained within a Blue Book. 'Beard Info' written in red pen on the front; essay in pencil edited in black pen.

items.title: Westlake Hills History: The Johnson Family
items.summary: Article about the Johnson family — particularly Charles Johnson and his wife Emilia Johnson.

items.title: Notes about W. L. Beard
items.summary: Transcription of a taped interview with W. L. Beard. Beard discusses his time in the Eanes County area, the dam break that forced people to abandon the riverside, and the construction of the Eanes Rock Schoolhouse.

items.title: Photograph of Della Edwards
items.summary: Della Edwards, the first Eanes ISD bus driver, standing next to an Eanes school bus with its doors open.

items.title: Westlake High School
items.summary: The front of Westlake High School with cars in the parking lot, trees, and a flag pole.

items.title: Photograph of Bruce Marshall
items.summary: Bruce Marshall at the Eanes-Marshall Cemetery, leaning on a fence with trees and a tombstone behind him.

items.title: Illustration of Eanes Frame School
items.summary: Art print of the Eanes Frame School in 1896 with two trees in front and a water tower beside it. Captioned '1896 frame school Eanes area used as a school 'till 1937 then a church 'till 1928'. Numbered 69/100; signed Curtis.

items.title: Report about the City of West Lake Hills
items.summary: Essay detailing the rise of the city of West Lake Hills — the founding of the Eanes School and the small businesses that helped build the town's infrastructure.

items.title: Bruce Marshall and Dorothy Depwe
items.summary: Bruce Marshall and Dorothy Depwe at the Eanes-Marshall Cemetery looking down at a tombstone, with multiple tombstones, trees, and shrubbery around them.

items.title: Photograph of Emma Beard and Friend
items.summary: Emma Beard and a friend in matching white-shirt-and-dark-skirt outfits, standing in front of a stone wall. Reverse: 'Emma Beard + friend 1950' in black pen.

items.title: Photograph of Eanes Rock Schoolhouse Students
items.summary: Seven boys and one girl posed in front of a brick building, all facing the camera; tan border around the photograph.

items.title: Typed Interview with Dan Molberg
items.summary: Interview with Dan Molberg on student life at Eanes Rock Schoolhouse in the 1940s — the 'big room' / 'little room' classes, daily meals, recess, PTA events, and fundraisers.

items.title: Cemeteries in Austin, Texas
items.summary: Photocopy of a list of ten cemeteries in the Austin area with brief descriptions of each.

items.title: Interview with James Short
items.summary: Transcript of an interview with James Short by Dorothy Depwe on growing up in Westlake Hills in the 1940s, including the famous bus 'egg fight'.

items.title: Growing up in Westlake Hills (James Short)
items.summary: Interview transcript with James Short, an Eanes Elementary alumnus, on incidents at school: bees on the school bus, a road bonfire built to stop the bus, and student fights.

items.title: Eanes Rock Schoolhouse Students
items.summary: Eanes Rock Schoolhouse students posed in front of the Eanes Rock Schoolhouse building. Reverse: cursive in pen, 'A 3 3' printed, 'gift from Betsy Linden Dyker 1939' in blue.

items.title: Photograph of the Johnson House
items.summary: Two-storey wooden house owned by Frank Johnson, with trees and bushes around it and a wood-and-wire fence in the foreground.

items.title: Interview with Eanes Rock Schoolhouse Alumni
items.summary: Interview with Myrtle Blankenship Treadwell, Pauline Becker, Ellen Seals, and Frank Spiller on Eanes Rock Schoolhouse in the 1930s and 40s — including the PTA-mothers' hot-lunch program and the displacement of Spiller Hill residents as the area was developed.

items.title: Recorded Conversation with Emmett Robert Shelton Sr.
items.summary: Oral history with Emmett Robert Shelton Sr. on the land development of Austin, the Brackenridge family, the laws passed in that period, and trading interactions with the Comanche.

items.title: Students Having Lunch Outside
items.summary: Eanes Rock School House students about to eat lunch outside, lined up and looking towards the camera. Reverse: 'R58' and 'APR 1940'.

items.title: Letter from Mary Mowinckle Johnson to Dorothy Depwe, February 12, 1976
items.summary: Letter from Mary Johnson to Stanley Depwe on a marker board added to the Eanes School. Includes a short biography of Mowinckle's life.

items.title: Westlake High School (1976)
items.summary: Westlake High School in 1976: the building, cars in the parking lot, two trees in the foreground.

items.title: Photograph of Humble Dealership
items.summary: The Humble Dealership and Gas Station next to Beard's store: two gas pumps in front, a 'HUMBLE DEALER' sign to the side, several windows in front, and a stone chimney to the right.

items.title: Interview with Gertrude Johnson
items.summary: Interview with Gertrude Johnson, a long-time resident of Deep Eddy, on her work with the church and school, a 17.5-pound sweet potato she once grew, and her years teaching in the Eanes area. Includes a recipe for 'Mrs. Johnson's unbaked cookies.'

items.title: Photograph of Eanes Rock Schoolhouse Students Outside
items.summary: Eanes Rock Schoolhouse students playing outside during a picnic, with a brick house to the left and trees in the picture. 'Gift from Betsy Linden Dyker' written on reverse in blue pen.

items.title: Essay about the Carlson Family
items.summary: Handwritten 9th-grade report on the Carlson family of the Eanes area — focused on Oscar Carlson, son of John Hendrik Carlson and a Swedish woman named Morning Star.

items.title: Interview with Rev. A. D. Eberhart
items.summary: Notes from an interview with Rev. A. D. Eberhart that include his age and notes about the Young and Brown family and guns.

items.title: Illustration of the Eanes-Marshall Home
items.summary: Art print of a two-storey house with outdoor stairs and a wooden fence. Captioned 'Eanes-Marshall Home Built 1857'. Numbered 69/100; signed Curtis.

items.title: Article for Westbank Picayune
items.summary: Article by Dona Price for Westbank Picayune on a new grocery store added to the Westbank area — a substantial change for the community whose nearest grocery had previously been miles away.

items.title: Oral History Interview with Mary Ellen Mowinckle Johnson, April 28, 1975
items.summary: Handwritten letter from Mary Johnson to Dorothy Depwe with an attached survey on life at the Eanes School and her teaching years there.

items.title: Note about the Jackson Family and Jackson Cemetery
items.summary: Handwritten notes about the Jackson family and Jackson Cemetery — including the landscape of the Eanes area and a smallpox epidemic that killed many Austin-area residents buried at Jackson Cemetery.

items.title: Illustration of Arch Bridge
items.summary: Art print of a stone bridge over a body of water, a horse-drawn carriage on top and people looking up from below. Captioned '1871 Arch Bridge over Barton Creek'. Numbered 69/100; signed Curtis.

items.title: Interview with Emmett Shelton and Benton Beard
items.summary: Transcription of an interview with Emmett Shelton and Benton Beard on the rise of the Eanes area, the Beard family, land development, the PTA, and Prohibition.

items.title: Interview with Earl Short
items.summary: Transcription of an interview with Earl Short by Dorothy Depwe. Short discusses multiple murders that occurred in the Westlake, Texas area as well as the lives of several local families.

items.title: Group of Boy Scouts
items.summary: Boy Scouts meeting at the Eanes Community Church — eight boys, the second-from-left in uniform, the rest in regular clothing. Reverse marked 'Eanes Community Church Boyscouts'. Damaged: tape and tears.

items.title: Students Playing in the Snow
items.summary: Two Eanes Rock Schoolhouse students playing in the snow — the boy is grabbing the girl's feet while she laughs at the camera. Brick house and trees in the background. Reverse: '6 B Eanes Students 1939-40'.

items.title: Typed Interview with Mary Ellen Mowinkle
items.summary: Mary Ellen Mowinckle (later Johnson) on her years teaching at Eanes Rock Schoolhouse — how she was hired, classroom discipline, and a measles outbreak that left only two students attending class.

items.title: Biography of Gertrude Johnson and Winnie Johnson
items.summary: Biography of Gertrude (Mrs. Frank A.) Johnson and Winnie (Mrs. E. W.) Johnson as founding members of the Westlake Hills Presbyterian Church in Austin. Notes their being awarded 'Honorary Life Memberships in the Women of the Church.'

items.title: Eanes Elementary Class Photograph
items.summary: Official Eanes Elementary class photograph for the yearbook — students arranged in four rows with the teacher standing to the right.

items.title: Eanes School Picnic
items.summary: Eanes Rock Schoolhouse students on a picnic at Smith Creek, seated on rocks surrounded by trees. Reverse: 'Eanes School Picnic 1930's'.

items.title: Photograph of Students in a Wagon
items.summary: Eanes Rock Schoolhouse students in a wagon, looking towards a man pointing to something off frame.

items.title: Interview with Mr. Elliot
items.summary: Transcription of an interview with Mr. Elliot on his upbringing at Noack's Crossing and his eventual move to Eanes County.

items.title: Handwritten Note about the Dellana Family
items.summary: Handwritten account by Dorothy Depwe of life on Tucker Lane in the 1920s — the Tucker family's children and the lives of the Teague, Ryan, Young, and Small families.

items.title: Portrait of Charles R. Eanes
items.summary: Portrait of Charles Randolph Eanes in a dark suit and vest with a black bow tie. Stamped on reverse: 'H. R. Marks Photographer, Austin, Texas.' Inscribed in blue ink: 'Charles Randolph Eanes 1887-1889.'

items.title: Photograph of West Lake High School
items.summary: Exterior of West Lake High School with cars in the parking lot and a tree in the foreground.

items.title: 1957 Travis County Rural Directory
items.summary: Directory and classified buyers' guide for Travis County: alphabetical resident and business listings, house numbers by road, farms by type, and a classified ads section.

items.title: 1958 Travis County Rural Directory
items.summary: Directory and classified buyers' guide for Travis County: alphabetical resident and business listings, house numbers by road, farms by type, and a classified ads section.

items.title: Echoes from the Past
items.summary: Brochure of historical information and stories about the Presbyterian church in the southern United States, presented at the 1936 Spring meeting of the Presbytery of Brazos in old First Church, Galveston.

items.title: Frontier Times, Volume 1, Number 3, December 1923
items.summary: Magazine of frontier history, border tragedy, and pioneer achievement — including articles on Navarro and Denton county colonists, a Texas Ranger's reminiscences, the capture of Cynthia Ann Parker, and James R. Moss of Llano.

items.title: The Texas Gulf Historical and Biographical Record, Volume 11, Number 1, November 1975
items.summary: Book of historical articles about Texas counties and towns and important historical figures.
tags: Texas Gulf Historical and Biographical Record

items.title: Eanes: A History of the School and Community
items.summary: Foundational text on the Eanes community and the history of the school district — covers the origins of the city of Austin, the development of the school, and includes transcribed supporting documents.

items.title: Eanes: Portrait of a Community
items.summary: Book history of the Eanes community area — geology, Native American history, the founding of the community in the mid-1800s, and development through 1986.
